summary: The reproduction bundle clones the pinned vulnerable snapshot, bootstraps a minimal OpenSTAManager state, logs in with a record-scoped shared token, and downloads an attachment from a different record by supplying file_id=2 to view.php.
expected_security_impact: A token that should only access module 1 record 1 should still be able to retrieve an unrelated attachment and reveal the proof string stolen-record-999.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the reproduction image, cloned the bundled repo snapshot at commit 1b717c4d36129a0f796555899915099a463cc280, bootstrapped the minimal database, authenticated with scope-token-123, and received HTTP 200 with filename="stolen.txt" and body stolen-record-999 from /view.php?file_id=2&download=1.
verification_method: The container first verifies the shared token redirects only to /editor.php?id_module=1&id_record=1, then requests view.php with file_id=2 and checks for both the stolen.txt download header and the exact body string stolen-record-999.
evidence_produced_by_run: stdout from ./reproduction/run.sh contained LOGIN_HEADERS_START with Location: /editor.php?id_module=1&id_record=1.
stdout from ./reproduction/run.sh contained DOWNLOAD_HEADERS_START with HTTP/1.1 200 OK and Content-disposition: attachment; filename="stolen.txt".
stdout from ./reproduction/run.sh contained DOWNLOAD_BODY_START followed by stolen-record-999.
stdout from ./reproduction/run.sh ended with EVOHUNT_SUCCESS scoped token downloaded out-of-scope attachment stolen.txt with body stolen-record-999.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from the bundled local git snapshot, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
